comments.text: An observation by GPT 5.5 pro : the known sufficient condition “positive natural density” can be weakened to positive upper Banach density. (Please see this for the full proof) Let\[ d^*(A)=\limsup_{L\to\infty}\sup_M \frac{|A\cap [M,M+L)|}{L}. \]If \(d^*(A)>0\), then \(D(A)\) has bounded gaps. Proof sketch. Put \(\delta=d^*(A)\), and let\[ P=\{h\in\mathbb Z: d^*(A\cap(A-h))>0\}. \]Then \(P\cap\mathbb N\subseteq D(A)\). I claim \(P\) is syndetic. Indeed, if \(t_0,\dots,t_m\in\mathbb Z\) and no nonzero difference \(t_i-t_j\) lies in \(P\), then the translates \(A-t_i\) have pairwise intersections of upper Banach density zero. Taking intervals on which \(A\) has density close to \(\delta\), and shifting them by only \(O_{t_i}(1)\), all \(A-t_i\) have density close to \(\delta\) on a common sequence of intervals. Inclusion-exclusion then gives\[ 1\ge (m+1)\delta-o(1). \]So \(m+1\le 1/\delta\). Thus any subset of \(\mathbb Z\) whose nonzero pairwise differences avoid \(P\) has size at most \(1/\delta\). Choose such a set \(F\) maximal. By maximality, every \(z\in\mathbb Z\) satisfies \(z-f\in P\) for some \(f\in F\), so \(P+F=\mathbb Z\). Hence \(P\) is syndetic. Therefore \(P\cap\mathbb N\), and hence \(D(A)\), has bounded gaps. This is not meant as a full characterization of the sets \(A\) for which \(D(A)\) has bounded gaps, just a slightly stronger sufficient condition.
